description_long: This is a robust, pedestal-style stone fountain set atop a square, multi-tiered brick base. The fountain bowl is circular with a thick, rounded rim and a central raised spout, suggesting a design for water to flow or bubble up from the middle. The pedestal is cylindrical, tapering slightly at the base, and is solidly constructed from what appears to be carved stone or concrete. The brick base consists of several ascending layers, each slightly smaller than the one below, forming a stepped pyramid effect that elevates the fountain. The surface of the stone shows some weathering and patina, indicating age and outdoor exposure, while the bricks are arranged in a neat, regular pattern. There are no visible water features or elaborate decorative motifs, giving the fountain a utilitarian yet classic appearance suitable for parks or public squares.
description: A classic stone pedestal fountain with a circular bowl and central spout, set on a stepped brick base. The design is sturdy and functional, with weathered stone and neatly arranged bricks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the engraved or inscribed side of the pedestal and the full stepped brick base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the smooth, unadorned surface of the pedestal and the symmetry of the brick steps.
description_view_2: Rear three-quarter view with the bowl and pedestal in shadow, emphasizing the depth and texture of the materials.
description_view_3: Back view with the pedestal and bowl mostly in shadow, showing the weathered stone and brickwork.
attribute_ornamentation_details: The fountain is largely unadorned, with a simple, utilitarian design. There may be faint engravings or inscriptions on the front of the pedestal, but no elaborate motifs or sculptural embellishments are visible.
attribute_basin_structure: The basin is a single, circular bowl with a thick, rounded rim and a central raised spout. It is moderately deep and lacks any visible tiering or internal compartments.
attribute_jet_or_spray_pattern: No visible jets or spray patterns; the central spout suggests a single upward water flow if operational.
